data.title: Update project docs to reflect per-ticket token attribution
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update observability documentation to describe per-ticket token attribution: how estimated and measured tokens are written, where to read them (`ticket show`), which skills emit estimates vs. which emit measurements, and how Jira sync will surface them (pointing at Epic 83e2-04ae).

**Why**: Without documentation, the new fields are invisible to users and downstream agents will not know to read them.

**Scope**:
- IN: Update `ticket-cli-reference.md` with the new `--tokens_estimated` and `--tokens_measured` flags on `ticket edit` and the new keys in `ticket show` output.
- IN: Update observability section in CLAUDE.md (or dedicated `plugins/dso/docs/OBSERVABILITY.md`) with the planning-vs-execution token model.
- IN: Example showing how to compute planning-cost ratio from a closed epic.
- OUT: Jira-side documentation — handled by Epic 83e2-04ae.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, users can read the documentation and understand (a) what tokens_estimated/tokens_measured mean, (b) which skills write them, (c) how to read them from the CLI, (d) the planning-vs-execution model.
  ← Satisfies: "Per-ticket token attribution"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` for formatting.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
